py_libraries.visualize.Figure module¶

class py_libraries.visualize.Figure.Figure(theme='paper', figsize=(8, 5), **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

Layout manager and output controller.

Usage¶

Single axes:

fig = Figure(theme=’paper’) ax = fig.addAxes(title=’Signal’, xlabel=’Time (s)’) ax.plot((t, y)).scatter((t, pts, {‘color’: ‘red’})) fig.show()

Subplots:

fig = Figure(theme=’paper’, figsize=(12, 8)) grid = fig.addSubplots(2, 3, sharex=True) grid[0][0].histogram(data) grid[0][1].scatter((x, y)) fig.save(‘results.pdf’)

Twin axes:

fig = Figure(theme=’paper’) ax1 = fig.addAxes(ylabel=’Temperature’) ax2 = fig.addTwin(ax1, ylabel=’Pressure’) ax1.plot((t, temp)) ax2.plot((t, pres, {‘color’: ‘red’})) fig.show()

Inset:

fig = Figure(theme=’paper’) ax = fig.addAxes() ins = fig.addInset(ax, bounds=(0.55, 0.55, 0.4, 0.35)) ax.plot((x, y)) ins.plot((x_zoom, y_zoom)) fig.show()

addAxes(**style_kwargs)[source]¶

Add a single axes filling the figure. Any AxisConfig field can be passed as a keyword argument.

Return type:

PlotAxes

addInset(source, bounds, **style_kwargs)[source]¶

Add an inset axes inside source.

Parameters:

  • bounds (Tuple[float, float, float, float])

  • source (PlotAxes)

Return type:

PlotAxes

addSubplots(nrows, ncols, sharex=False, sharey=False, hspace=0.35, wspace=0.3)[source]¶

Create a (nrows x ncols) grid of axes. Returns a 2D list; access panels as grid[row][col].

Parameters:

  • sharex (bool) – All axes in each column share the same x-axis.

  • sharey (bool) – All axes in each row share the same y-axis.

  • hspace (float) – Vertical spacing between rows (in fraction of average axis height).

  • wspace (float) – Horizontal spacing between columns.

  • nrows (int)

  • ncols (int)

Return type:

List[List[PlotAxes]]

addTwin(source, axis='x', **style_kwargs)[source]¶

Add an axis that shares the x-axis (axis=’x’) or y-axis (axis=’y’) with source.

The new axis gets its own independent scale on the opposite side. Any AxisConfig field (e.g. ylabel=’Pressure’) can be passed as a keyword argument.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

  • source (PlotAxes)

  • axis (str)

close()[source]¶

Close the figure and release its memory.

Return type:

None

property raw: Figure¶

Underlying matplotlib Figure, for any operation not covered above.

save(path, dpi=150)[source]¶

Render and write the figure to disk.

Return type:

None

Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • dpi (int)

show()[source]¶

Render and display the figure interactively.

Return type:

None

Parameters:

  • theme (Union[str, Theme])

  • figsize (Tuple[float, float])

class py_libraries.visualize.Figure.PlotAxes(ax, config=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Fluent wrapper around matplotlib Axes.

All plot methods return self to allow chaining:

ax.plot(data).scatter(pts).style(title=’Result’, legend=True)

Style configuration is applied lazily: call render() explicitly, or let Figure.show() / Figure.save() trigger it automatically.

Parameters:

  • ax (MplAxes)

  • config (Optional[AxisConfig])

bar(*args, text=False, fmt='{}', **kwargs)[source]¶

Bar chart. Set text=True to annotate bar heights; use fmt to control format.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

  • text (bool)

  • fmt (str)

candles(data, width=0.6, open_color='green', close_color='red', wick_width=1.0, show_legend=True, **kwargs)[source]¶

OHLC candlestick chart.

Parameters:

  • data (array of shape (N, 4) -- columns: open, high, low, close.)

  • width (float)

  • open_color (str)

  • close_color (str)

  • wick_width (float)

  • show_legend (bool)

Return type:

PlotAxes

ecdf(x, **kwargs)[source]¶

Empirical cumulative distribution function.

Return type:

PlotAxes

errorbar(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Error bar plot. Each series is plotted as mean +/- std.

Return type:

PlotAxes

heatmap(*args, bins=100, cmap='viridis', cbar_label='', intensity=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

2D density heatmap. Pass intensity=array for weighted bin averaging.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

  • bins (int)

  • cmap (str)

  • cbar_label (str)

histogram(data, bins=None, bin_type='linear', **kwargs)[source]¶

Univariate histogram.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

  • bins (int | None)

  • bin_type (str)

image(*args, colorbar=False, cbar_label='', **kwargs)[source]¶

Display one or more images.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

  • colorbar (bool)

  • cbar_label (str)

plot(*args, cmap=None, cmap_label='', cmap_values=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Line plot.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

  • cmap (str | None)

  • cmap_label (str)

property raw: Axes¶

Underlying matplotlib Axes, for any operation not covered above.

render()[source]¶

Apply AxisConfig to the underlying matplotlib Axes.

Return type:

PlotAxes

scatter(*args, cmap_label=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter plot. Accepts c=array, cmap=’…’ for color-mapping.

Return type:

PlotAxes

Parameters:

cmap_label (str | None)

scatterDensity(*args, density_alpha=False, center=False, rate=False, bw_method=None, cbar_label=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter plot colored by 2D Gaussian KDE density.

Parameters:

  • density_alpha (bool) – Scale point opacity by local density.

  • center (Union[bool, float]) – Restrict the view to the densest region. True = 90%. A value in (0, 1] is a fraction; a value > 1 is a percentage.

  • rate (Union[bool, float, int]) – Discard low-density points before plotting. Float: keep the top fraction. Int: keep the top N points.

  • bw_method (Union[float, str, None]) – Bandwidth selector forwarded to scipy.stats.gaussian_kde.

  • cbar_label (Optional[str]) – Colorbar label. Pass None to suppress the colorbar.

Return type:

PlotAxes

style(**kwargs)[source]¶

Update axis configuration. Accepts any AxisConfig field as a keyword argument.

Example: ax.style(title=’Signal’, xlabel=’Time (s)’, legend=True)

Return type:

PlotAxes

py_libraries.visualize.color module¶

class py_libraries.visualize.color.Color[source]¶

Bases: object

static all()[source]¶

All named colors from matplotlib (base, CSS4, Tableau, XKCD).

static base()[source]¶

Base colors (short names).

static complementary(color_name)[source]¶

Return hex code for the complementary color of a named color.

static css4()[source]¶

CSS4 named colors.

static dark(threshold=0.5)[source]¶

Named colors with luminance at or below threshold (dark colors).

static darkNamed()[source]¶

Named colors whose names start with ‘dark’ (case-insensitive).

static darken(color_name, factor=0.2)[source]¶

Darken a named color by a given factor (0..1).

static grayscale(tolerance=1e-06)[source]¶

Named colors where R≈G≈B within tolerance.

static hex_values()[source]¶

All hex values of named colors.

static light()[source]¶

Named colors whose names start with ‘light’ (case-insensitive).

static lighten(color_name, factor=0.2)[source]¶

Lighten a named color by a given factor (0..1).

static luminance(color_name)[source]¶

Return relative luminance [0..1] of a named color.

static other()[source]¶

Colors not in base, tableau, CSS4, or XKCD.

static primary(dominance=0.1)[source]¶

Colors where one channel exceeds the other two by ‘dominance’.

static random(n=5, seed=None)[source]¶

Random subset of n named colors.

static sorted_by_hue(include_hex=False)[source]¶

Return colors sorted by HSV hue. Optionally include hex codes.

static tableau()[source]¶

Tableau colors (named palette colors).

static verify()[source]¶

Verify that named mappings are consistent and unique.

static xkcd()[source]¶

XKCD survey colors (prefixed names).

py_libraries.visualize.main module¶

py_libraries.visualize.main.isClose(ax)[source]¶

bool

Parameters:

ax (Axes)

py_libraries.visualize.main.isOpen(ax)[source]¶
Return type:

bool

Parameters:

ax (Axes)

py_libraries.visualize.main.makeBins(data, bins=100, bin_type='linear')[source]¶

Create histogram bin edges with different spacing types.

Parameters:

  • data (array-like) – Source data; used to infer range when bins is an integer.

  • bins (int) – Number of bins (int) or explicit base edges (array-like).

  • bin_type (str) – Spacing strategy: ‘linear’ (default), ‘log’, ‘sqrt’, ‘quantile’. ‘quantile’ is only valid when bins is an integer.

Returns:

Array of bin edges.

Return type:

ndarray

py_libraries.visualize.main.save(fig, file_path, dpi=150)[source]¶
Return type:

None

Parameters:

  • file_path (str)

  • dpi (int)

bool

Parameters:

ax (Axes)

py_libraries.visualize.main_deprecate.isOpen(ax)[source]¶
Return type:

bool

Parameters:

ax (Axes)

py_libraries.visualize.main_deprecate.makeBins(data, bins=100, bin_type='linear')[source]¶

Create histogram bin edges with different spacing types.

Parameters - data : array-like

The data from which to infer min/max values (used only if bins is int).

binsint or array-like

  • If int: defines the number of bins to generate.

  • If array-like: base edges to which the bin_type transformation is applied.

bin_typestr, optional

Type of bin spacing: - “linear” : no transformation (default) - “log” : logarithmic spacing - “sqrt” : square-root spacing - “quantile” : equal number of samples (ignored if bins are array-like)

Returns - : np.ndarray

Array of bin edges.

Parameters:

bin_type (str)

py_libraries.visualize.main_deprecate.save(fig, file_path, dpi=200)[source]¶

py_libraries.visualize.plot_deprecate module¶

class py_libraries.visualize.plot_deprecate.Plot[source]¶

Bases: object

Unified plotting interface. All methods accept: - *args: each can be

  • y-array

  • (x, y)

  • (x, y, {opts})

  • fig, ax: pass existing fig/ax if desired.

  • path (opt), show (bool)

  • global styling kwargs: title, xlabel, ylabel, grid, xlim, ylim, legend, xscale, yscale

static bar(*args, text=False, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bar chart.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static candles(data, *, width=0.6, open_color='green', close_color='red', wick_width=1.0, legend=True, **kwargs)[source]¶

OHLC candlestick chart. data is an (N,4) array of [open, high, low, close].

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • data (ndarray)

  • width (float)

  • open_color (str)

  • close_color (str)

  • wick_width (float)

  • legend (bool)

static ecdf(x, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

empirical cumulative distribution function of x

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static errorbar(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Error bar plot using per-series mean and standard deviation.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static heatmap(*args, bins=100, cmap='viridis', **kwargs)[source]¶

2D density heatmap.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • bins (int)

  • cmap (str)

static histogram(data, bins=None, bin_type='linear', **kwargs)[source]¶

Univariate histogram.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • data (List | ndarray)

  • bins (int | None)

static image(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Display an image or a set of images.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static plot(*args, cmap=None, cmap_label='', cmap_values=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Simple line plot.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static scatter(*args, cmap_label=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter / dot plot. scatter already accept: c=cmap_values, cmap=’viridis’

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

static scatterDensity(*args, density_alpha=False, center=False, rate=False, bw_method=None, cbar_label=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Scatter plot colored by a 2D Gaussian KDE estimate of density.

Return type:

Optional[Figure]

Parameters:

  • bw_method (str | float | None)

  • cbar_label (str | None)

Parameters: - *args: each of

  • y-array

  • (x, y)

  • (x, y, {opts})

  • bw_method: passed to gaussian_kde (None for scott’s rule, or float/string)

  • cbar_label: label for the density colorbar. If none no color bar

  • plus all the usual kwargs (fig, ax, title, xlabel, path, show, etc.)

  • center: If true, center (the last plot, useless if many args) on the 90% denser values. If a number on it’s pourcentage or rate
